explanation: Appendix 2, ITEM 19, D/ (DINGHIES): 'C if dinghies are covered.'

question_text: In Appendix 3, Field Type 3, what is the meaning of an open bracket '(' preceding the message type designator?
explanation: Appendix 3, 1.5.1: 'The beginning of the ATS data shall be indicated on page copy by an open bracket '(', which constitutes the Start-of-ATS-Data Signal.'

question_text: In Appendix 3, Field Type 14 (Estimate data), what does the letter 'A' in the crossing condition indicate?

options.A: Aircraft will cross at or above the level
options.B: Aircraft will cross at or below the level
options.C: Aircraft will cross at the exact level
options.D: Aircraft will cross after the level
options.E: Aircraft will cross before the level
options.F: Aircraft will cross at any level

explanation: Appendix 3, Field Type 14, element (e) Crossing condition: '1 LETTER as follows: A if the aircraft will cross the boundary point at or above the level in (d), or B if the aircraft will cross the boundary point at or below the level in (d).'

question_text: In Appendix 3, Field Type 22 (Amendment), what is the correct format to amend Field Type 8 to 'IN'?
explanation: Appendix 3, Field Type 22 example: 'Example of amendment of Field Type 8 (Flight rules and type of flight) to IN: –8/IN'

question_text: In Appendix 4, what is the purpose of the diagram section on the air traffic incident report form?

options.A: To show the aircraft's position relative to ground obstacles
options.B: To mark passage of other aircraft relative to your aircraft in plan and elevation, including first sighting and passing distance
options.D: To show the route of flight

explanation: Appendix 4, after the diagram: 'Mark passage of other aircraft relative to you, in plan on the left and in elevation on the right, assuming YOU are at the centre of each diagram. Include first sighting and passing distance.'

question_text: In Appendix 5, what is the response attribute for uplink message element 'LVLU-1' (EXPECT HIGHER AT TIME)?
explanation: Table A5-3-1 shows for LVLU-1 the RESP (response) column is 'R', which according to Table 14-2 means the valid responses include ROGER, UNABLE, STANDBY, etc.

question_text: What is the CPDLC uplink message element for 'MAINTAIN PRESENT SPEED'?
explanation: Table A5-5-1, Speed uplinks: SPDU-5 is 'MAINTAIN PRESENT SPEED'.

question_text: In Appendix 5, what does the message element 'EMGD-2' represent?

options.A: Indication of an urgent situation (PAN PAN)

explanation: Table A5-9-2, Emergency/urgency downlinks: EMGD-2 is 'MAYDAY MAYDAY MAYDAY' indicating an emergency situation.

question_text: In Appendix 6, what is the purpose of the 'Transfer Communication Request' message?

options.A: To indicate that the transferring unit has instructed the flight to establish communication with the accepting unit
options.B: An unsolicited request by the accepting unit to establish communication with the flight immediately
options.C: To propose transfer of control conditions
options.E: To cancel a previous transfer request

explanation: Appendix 6, paragraph 2.12: 'Transfer Communication Request message is an unsolicited request by the controller in the accepting unit to establish communication with the flight on the appropriate channel immediately.'

question_text: In Appendix 6, which AIDC message is used to update the receiving unit with current control environment data such as cleared flight level, speed restrictions, etc., before transfer?
explanation: Appendix 6, paragraph 2.9: 'The Transfer Initiate message satisfies the following operational requirements: a) informs the receiving unit of the current control environment of the flight; and b) alleviates the requirement for the controller in the transferring unit to verbally provide this information...' Also 2.9 describes that it contains executive data.

question_text: In Appendix 6, what is the mandatory content of a 'Coordinate Cancel' message?

options.A: Aircraft identification, departure aerodrome, destination aerodrome, fix, and reason for cancellation

explanation: Table A6-1 shows for Coordinate Cancel: mandatory fields: Aircraft identification, Departure aerodrome, Destination aerodrome, Fix, Reason for cancellation.

question_text: According to Chapter 4, what is the maximum speed for air-taxiing of a helicopter?
explanation: The definition of 'Air-taxiing' states: 'Movement of a helicopter/VTOL above the surface of an aerodrome, normally in ground effect and at a ground speed normally less than 37 km/h (20 kt).'

question_text: What is the minimum vertical separation for emergency separation when the normal VSM is 300 m (1000 ft)?
explanation: Chapter 15, 15.7.1.1: 'If, during an emergency situation, it is not possible to ensure that the applicable horizontal separation can be maintained, emergency separation of half the applicable vertical separation minimum may be used, i.e. 150 m (500 ft) between aircraft in airspace where a vertical separation minimum of 300 m (1 000 ft) is applied.'

question_text: What is the minimum altitude for fuel dumping, if possible?
explanation: Chapter 15, 15.5.3.1.2 b): 'the level to be used, which should be not less than 1 800 m (6 000 ft).'

question_text: What is the maximum number of mandatory RNAV and/or RNP descriptors that can be specified under the PBN/ indicator in Item 18?
explanation: Appendix 2, ITEM 18, PBN/ indicator: 'Include as many of the descriptors below, as apply to the flight, up to a maximum of 8 entries, i.e. a total of not more than 16 characters.'

question_text: What is the correct altimeter setting procedure for an aircraft climbing through the transition layer?

options.A: Express vertical position in terms of altitudes
options.B: Express vertical position in terms of flight levels
options.C: Express vertical position in terms of altitudes when climbing and flight levels when descending
options.D: Express vertical position in terms of flight levels when climbing and altitudes when descending
options.E: Express vertical position in QFE only
options.F: Express vertical position in height above aerodrome

explanation: The document states: 'While passing through the transition layer, vertical position shall be expressed in terms of flight levels when climbing and in terms of altitudes when descending.' (Chapter 4, 4.10.1.1)

question_text: When shall a QFE altimeter setting be provided for a precision approach runway?

options.B: On a regular basis in accordance with local arrangements or on request
options.C: Only when the threshold is 2 m or more below the aerodrome elevation
options.D: Always, as the QFE for the relevant runway threshold

explanation: The document states: 'A QFE altimeter setting shall be provided to aircraft on request or on a regular basis in accordance with local arrangements; it shall be the QFE for the aerodrome elevation except for: a) non-precision approach runways, if the threshold is 2m (7 ft) or more below the aerodrome elevation; and b) precision approach runways; in which cases the QFE for the relevant runway threshold shall be provided.' (Chapter 4, 4.10.4.6)

question_text: What is the maximum time after the expected time of a position report that a controller should take action to obtain the report if it is likely to affect control of other aircraft?
explanation: The document states: 'If a position report is not received at the expected time, subsequent control shall not be based on the assumption that the estimated time is accurate. Immediate action shall be taken to obtain the report if it is likely to have any bearing on the control of other aircraft.' (Chapter 4, 4.11.1.5)

question_text: What data block is mandatory in all ADS-C reports?

options.C: Basic ADS-C (latitude, longitude, altitude, time, figure of merit)

explanation: The document states: 'Note.- The basic ADS-C block is mandatory and is included in all ADS-C reports.' (Chapter 4, 4.11.5.1 b) Note)

question_text: What action should be taken by an ATC unit when receiving special air-reports by voice concerning braking action that is not as good as reported?

options.A: Forward them without delay to the associated meteorological watch office
options.B: Forward them without delay to the appropriate aerodrome operator
options.C: Forward them to the world area forecast centres
options.D: Record them and take no further action
options.F: Forward them to the next ATC unit

explanation: The document states: 'When receiving special air-reports by voice communications concerning braking action encountered that is not as good as reported, air traffic service units shall forward them without delay to the appropriate aerodrome operator.' (Chapter 4, 4.12.7)

question_text: What is the minimum period for which paper flight progress strips (FPS) must be retained for investigative purposes?
explanation: The document states: 'Paper FPS shall be retained for a period of at least 30 days. Electronic flight progress and coordination data shall be recorded and retained for at least the same period of time.' (Chapter 4, 4.13.4)

question_text: What is the initial action by an aircraft for data link initiation when entering airspace where data link applications are used by the ATS unit?

options.A: Wait for the ATS unit to initiate
options.B: Data link communications shall be initiated by the aircraft, either automatically or by the pilot, or by the ATS unit on address forwarding
options.C: Only the ATS unit can initiate
options.D: Only voice communications are used initially
options.E: The pilot must request via voice first

explanation: The document states: 'Before entering an airspace where data link applications are used by the ATS unit, data link communications shall be initiated between the aircraft and the ATS unit in order to register the aircraft and, when necessary, allow the start of a data link application. This shall be initiated by the aircraft, either automatically or by the pilot, or by the ATS unit on address forwarding.' (Chapter 4, 4.15.1.1)

question_text: What is the minimum vertical separation between a helicopter and light aircraft when the helicopter is hovering or air taxiing?

options.C: No specific minimum, but helicopters should be kept well clear of light aircraft
options.F: Same as fixed-wing wake turbulence minima

explanation: The document states: 'Helicopters should be kept well clear of light aircraft when hovering or while air taxiing.' (Chapter 4, 4.9.1.3) No specific numerical separation is given; it is a qualitative requirement.

question_text: When may a reduced separation minimum be implemented according to Chapter 5?

options.A: Whenever the controller deems it safe
options.B: Provided an appropriate safety risk assessment has shown an acceptable level of safety will be maintained, and after prior consultation with users

explanation: The document states: 'Provided an appropriate safety risk assessment has shown that an acceptable level of safety will be maintained, and after prior consultation with users, the separation minima detailed in 5.4.1 and 5.4.2 may be reduced in the following circumstances:' (Chapter 5, 5.11.1)

question_text: What is the maximum crosswind component, including gusts, for which noise abatement shall not be a determining factor in runway nomination?
explanation: The document states: 'Noise abatement shall not be a determining factor in runway nomination under the following circumstances: ... e) when the crosswind component, including gusts, exceeds 28 km/h (15 kt), or the tailwind component, including gusts, exceeds 9 km/h (5 kt).' (Chapter 7, 7.2.6 e))

question_text: What is the correct procedure for a pilot who is in doubt as to the position of the aircraft with respect to the manoeuvring area and recognizes that the aircraft is on a runway?

options.B: Notify ATC, vacate the runway if able to a suitable taxiway, then stop
options.C: Continue taxiing until a safe area is reached
options.E: Turn on all lights and await instructions
options.F: Backtrack on the runway to the last known position

explanation: The document states: 'In those situations where a pilot is in doubt as to the position of the aircraft with respect to the manoeuvring area, but recognizes that the aircraft is on a runway, the pilot shall immediately: a) notify the appropriate ATS unit of the circumstances (including the last known position); b) if able to locate a nearby suitable taxiway, vacate the runway as expeditiously as possible, unless otherwise instructed by the ATS unit; and then, c) stop the aircraft.' (Chapter 7, 7.4.1.5.2)

question_text: What is the minimum distance that reduced runway separation minima for a Category 1 aircraft landing behind a Category 1 or 2 aircraft requires the preceding aircraft to have passed from the threshold?
explanation: The document states: 'a succeeding landing Category 1 aircraft may cross the runway threshold when the preceding aircraft is a Category 1 or 2 aircraft which either: i) has landed and has passed a point at least 600 m from the threshold of the runway, is in motion and will vacate the runway without backtracking; or ii) is airborne and has passed a point at least 600 m from the threshold of the runway.' (Chapter 7, 7.11.7 a) 1))

question_text: What is the maximum range scale or condition under which PSR may be used alone for identification?

options.B: Only within 10 NM of the radar site
options.C: Only when the aircraft reports its position over a point shown on the display and the track is consistent
options.D: Only when SSR is also available

explanation: The document describes PSR identification procedures: 'by correlating a particular radar position indication with an aircraft reporting its position over, or as bearing and distance from, a point shown on the situation display, and by ascertaining that the track of the particular radar position is consistent with the aircraft path or reported heading' (Chapter 8, 8.6.2.4.1 a)). The appropriate ATS authority may prescribe additional conditions such as maximum distance from the radar site or above certain levels.

question_text: What is the required action when the SSR Mode A code shown on the situation display is different from what has been assigned?

options.A: Immediately request the pilot to stop transponder operation
options.B: Request the pilot to confirm the code selected and, if warranted, to reselect the correct code
options.D: Change the assigned code in the flight plan

explanation: The document states: 'When it is observed that the Mode A code shown on the situation display is different to what has been assigned to the aircraft, the pilot shall be requested to confirm the code selected and, if the situation warrants (e.g. not being a case of unlawful interference), to reselect the correct code.' (Chapter 8, 8.5.3.1)

question_text: What is the criterion for determining that an aircraft has reached a level for the purpose of level occupancy using pressure-altitude information?

options.A: When the altimeter reading matches the assigned level exactly
options.B: When the elapsed time of three display updates, three sensor updates or 15 seconds, whichever is greater, has passed since the pressure-altitude-derived level information indicates it is within the appropriate tolerance of the assigned level
options.C: When the pilot reports reaching the level
options.D: When the aircraft passes the level by 200 ft
options.E: When the controller observes the level change on display
options.F: When the transponder indicates a change

explanation: The document states: 'An aircraft is considered to have reached the level to which it has been cleared when the elapsed time of three display updates, three sensor updates or 15 seconds, whichever is the greater, has passed since the pressure-altitude-derived level information has indicated that it is within the appropriate tolerances of the assigned level, as specified in 8.5.5.2.1.' (Chapter 8, 8.5.5.2.5)

question_text: What is the minimum vectoring altitude consideration when vectoring an IFR flight off an ATS route?

options.A: The controller must ensure the prescribed obstacle clearance will exist at all times until the pilot resumes own navigation
options.C: The pilot is solely responsible for obstacle clearance
options.D: The aircraft must be vectored only above FL 290
options.E: Vectoring is not permitted off ATS routes
options.F: The controller must coordinate with the pilot's company

explanation: The document states: 'When vectoring an IFR flight and when giving an IFR flight a direct routing which takes the aircraft off an ATS route, the controller shall issue clearances such that the prescribed obstacle clearance will exist at all times until the aircraft reaches the point where the pilot will resume own navigation. When necessary, the relevant minimum vectoring altitude shall include a correction for low temperature effect.' (Chapter 8, 8.6.5.2)

question_text: What action should a controller take when an unidentified controlled flight enters airspace where radar separation minima are applied?

options.B: Continue providing radar service to identified aircraft provided reasonable assurance exists that the unidentified flight will be identified using SSR/ADS-B/MLAT or gives adequate primary radar return, and separation is maintained between identified flights and any other observed position indications until identified or procedural separation is established
options.C: Stop all radar services until the unidentified flight is identified
options.D: Transfer control to the adjacent unit
options.E: Instruct all aircraft to squawk standby

explanation: The document states: 'In the event that the controller has been notified of a controlled flight entering or about to enter the airspace within which the separation minima specified in 8.7.3 is applied, but has not identified the aircraft, the controller may, if so prescribed by the appropriate ATS authority, continue to provide an ATS surveillance service to identified aircraft provided that: a) reasonable assurance exists that the unidentified controlled flight will be identified using SSR and/or ADS-B and/or MLAT or the flight is being operated by an aircraft of a type which may be expected to give an adequate return on primary radar in the airspace within which the separation is applied; and b) the separation is maintained between identified flights and any other observed ATS surveillance system position indications until either the unidentified controlled flight has been identified or procedural separation has been established.' (Chapter 8, 8.7.2.8)

question_text: What is the minimum horizontal separation for radar when reduced to 2.5 NM, under what condition?

options.A: Between succeeding aircraft established on the same final approach track within 10 NM of the runway threshold, with conditions on runway occupancy time, braking action, surveillance system update rate, and controller observation
options.B: Between any aircraft at any time if radar accuracy permits

explanation: The document states: 'b) 4.6 km (2.5 NM) between succeeding aircraft which are established on the same final approach track within 18.5 km (10 NM) of the runway threshold. A reduced separation minimum of 4.6 km (2.5 NM) may be applied, provided: i) the average runway occupancy time of landing aircraft is proven ... not to exceed 50 seconds; ii) braking action is reported as good ...; iii) an ATS surveillance system with appropriate azimuth and range resolution and an update rate of 5 seconds or less is used ...; iv) the aerodrome controller is able to observe ... the runway-in-use and associated exit and entry taxiways; v) distance-based wake turbulence separation minima ... do not apply; vi) aircraft approach speeds are closely monitored; vii) aircraft operators and pilots have been made fully aware of the need to exit the runway in an expeditious manner; and viii) procedures concerning the application of the reduced minimum are published in AIPs.' (Chapter 8, 8.7.3.2 b))

question_text: What is the distance-based wake turbulence separation minimum between a GROUP A preceding aircraft and a GROUP G succeeding aircraft?
explanation: Table 8.7.3.5 shows for preceding aircraft group A and succeeding aircraft group G: 14.9 km (8.0 NM).

question_text: What is the maximum time allowed for a controller to intervene and resolve a potential conflict using the normal means of communication when applying performance-based longitudinal separation minima?
explanation: The document states: 'The communication system provided to enable the application of the separation minima in 5.4.2.9.2 shall allow a controller, within 4 minutes, to intervene and resolve a potential conflict by contacting an aircraft using the normal means of communication.' (Chapter 5, 5.4.2.9.6)

question_text: What is the maximum permissible variation for reporting a revised expected approach time to an aircraft?
explanation: The document states: 'A revised expected approach time shall be transmitted to the aircraft without delay whenever it differs from that previously transmitted by 5 minutes or more, or such lesser period of time as has been established by the appropriate ATS authority or agreed between the ATS units concerned.' (Chapter 6, 6.5.7.1)

question_text: What is the minimum angle of divergence for missed approach tracks for independent parallel approaches?
explanation: The document states: 'the nominal tracks of the missed approach procedures diverge by at least 30 degrees;' (Chapter 6, 6.7.3.2.1 c))

question_text: When are independent parallel approaches to closely spaced parallel runways (less than 1525 m) suspended?

options.B: Under certain meteorological conditions prescribed by the ATS authority, such as wind shear, turbulence, crosswind, and thunderstorms
options.E: When military operations are in progress

explanation: The document states: 'Independent parallel approaches to parallel runways spaced by less than 1525 m between their centre lines shall be suspended under certain meteorological conditions, as prescribed by the appropriate ATS authority, including wind shear, turbulence, downdrafts, crosswind and significant meteorological conditions such as thunderstorms, which might otherwise increase deviations from the final approach course or track to the extent that safety may be impaired.' (Chapter 6, 6.7.3.3)

question_text: What is the maximum height above the runway threshold elevation below which a heading instruction shall not be issued to an aircraft during a break-out procedure for independent parallel approaches where PAOAS criteria are applied?
explanation: The document states: 'Where parallel approach obstacle assessment surfaces (PAOAS) criteria are applied for the obstacle assessment, the monitoring controller shall not issue the heading instruction to the aircraft below 120 m (400 ft) above the runway threshold elevation, and the heading instruction shall not exceed 45 degrees track difference with the final approach course or track.' (Chapter 6, 6.7.3.2.10)

question_text: What is the requirement for vehicle communication at controlled aerodromes?

options.A: All vehicles on the manoeuvring area must be capable of two-way radiocommunication with the tower, except when only occasionally used and accompanied by a vehicle with communication or employed under a pre-arranged plan
options.D: Only vehicles with flashing lights need communication
options.E: Only vehicles towing aircraft need communication
options.F: Vehicles are not permitted on the manoeuvring area

explanation: The document states: 'At controlled aerodromes all vehicles employed on the manoeuvring area shall be capable of maintaining two-way radiocommunication with the aerodrome control tower, except when the vehicle is only occasionally used on the manoeuvring area and is: a) accompanied by a vehicle with the required communications capability; or b) employed in accordance with a pre-arranged plan established with the aerodrome control tower.' (Chapter 7, 7.6.3.2.3.1)

question_text: What is the meaning of the phrase 'TAXI INTO HOLDING BAY'?

options.A: Taxi to the runway holding point
options.B: Taxi into a designated parking area or bay off the taxiway

explanation: Under taxi procedures, the phrase 'TAXI INTO HOLDING BAY' is listed. A holding bay is an area where aircraft can wait off the taxiway, typically before the runway holding point. It is a designated area to hold short of the active runway.

question_text: What is the phraseology to instruct an aircraft to stop a take-off after it has commenced take-off roll?

options.B: STOP IMMEDIATELY, (repeat aircraft call sign) STOP IMMEDIATELY

explanation: The document lists: '... to stop a take-off after an aircraft has commenced take-off roll g) STOP IMMEDIATELY [(repeat aircraft call sign) STOP IMMEDIATELY];' (Chapter 12, 12.3.4.11)

question_text: What is the phraseology to instruct an aircraft to make a three-sixty turn?

options.B: MAKE A THREE SIXTY TURN LEFT (or RIGHT) [reason]

explanation: Under ATS surveillance phraseologies: 'a) MAKE A THREE SIXTY TURN LEFT (or RIGHT) [reason];' (Chapter 12, 12.4.1.5)

question_text: What is the phraseology for an aircraft to report that it is established on the localizer?
explanation: Under radar in approach control service: 'e) REPORT ESTABLISHED ON LOCALIZER (or ON [GLS/RNP/MLS] [FINAL] APPROACH [COURSE]);' (Chapter 12, 12.4.2.2 e))

question_text: What is the phraseology to request an aircraft to confirm that it is RVSM approved?
explanation: Under RVSM operations phraseology: 'a) CONFIRM RVSM APPROVED;' (Chapter 12, 12.3.1.13)

question_text: What is the meaning of the phrase 'CAVOK' in meteorological information?

options.C: No significant weather, ceiling and visibility OK
options.D: Ceiling above 5,000 ft and visibility more than 10 km, no precipitation

explanation: The phrase 'CAVOK' is listed under meteorological conditions phraseologies. While the document does not expand on its meaning, it is standard aviation phraseology for 'Ceiling and Visibility OK' (no significant weather, visibility 10 km or more, no clouds below 5,000 ft or MSA, no precipitation, thunderstorms, etc.).

question_text: What is the required action for an ATS unit when a flight ceases to be a controlled flight (e.g., leaving controlled airspace or cancelling IFR)?

options.B: Ensure that appropriate information on the flight is forwarded to the ATS unit(s) responsible for flight information and alerting services for the remaining portion
options.C: Continue to provide control service until the flight lands
options.D: Request the pilot to file a new flight plan

explanation: The document states: 'In the case where a flight ceases to be operated as a controlled flight, i.e. by leaving controlled airspace or by cancelling its IFR flight and proceeding on VFR in airspace where VFR flights are not controlled, the ATC unit concerned shall ensure that appropriate information on the flight is forwarded to ATS unit(s) responsible for the provision of flight information and alerting services for the remaining portion of the flight, in order to ensure that such services will be provided to the aircraft.' (Chapter 10, 10.1.2.5)

question_text: What is the minimum lead time for forwarding information on arriving aircraft from an ACC to an approach control unit?
explanation: The document states: 'Information on arriving aircraft shall be forwarded not less than fifteen minutes before estimated time of arrival and such information shall be revised as necessary.' (Chapter 10, 10.1.3.3.3)

question_text: What is the definition of 'strayed aircraft'?

options.A: An aircraft which has deviated significantly from its intended track or which reports that it is lost
options.B: An aircraft that has been intercepted
options.C: An aircraft that has declared an emergency
options.D: An aircraft that has experienced communication failure
options.E: An aircraft that is not identified on radar
options.F: An aircraft that has been unlawfully interfered with

explanation: The document states: 'Strayed aircraft. An aircraft which has deviated significantly from its intended track or which reports that it is lost.' (Chapter 15, 15.5.1 Note 1)

question_text: What is the primary objective of the minimum safe altitude warning (MSAW) function?

options.B: To assist in the prevention of controlled flight into terrain accidents
options.D: To alert controllers of unauthorized runway occupancy

explanation: The document states: 'The objective of the MSAW function is to assist in the prevention of controlled flight into terrain accidents by generating, in a timely manner, a warning of the possible infringement of a minimum safe altitude.' (Chapter 15, 15.7.4 Note 1)

question_text: What action should a controller take when an aircraft reports an ACAS resolution advisory (RA) that requires deviation from clearance?

options.A: Immediately issue new instructions to resolve the conflict
options.B: Not attempt to modify the aircraft flight path until the pilot reports 'Clear of Conflict'
options.C: Ask the pilot to disregard the RA

explanation: The document states: 'When a pilot reports an ACAS resolution advisory (RA), the controller shall not attempt to modify the aircraft flight path until the pilot reports 'Clear of Conflict'.' (Chapter 15, 15.7.3.2)

question_text: For a flight plan received from an aircraft in flight, what is inserted in Item 13 instead of a departure aerodrome?
explanation: Appendix 2, ITEM 13 instructions: 'INSERT AFIL if the flight plan has been filed in the air.'

question_text: What is the correct way to indicate a significant point defined by bearing and distance from a VOR in the route field?

options.B: DUB180040 (for a point 180° magnetic at 40 NM from DUB)

explanation: Appendix 2, Item 15 (c)(2) Significant point: 'Bearing and distance from a reference point: The identification of the reference point, followed by the bearing from the point in the form of 3 figures giving degrees magnetic, followed by the distance from the point in the form of 3 figures expressing nautical miles. ... e.g. a point 180° magnetic at a distance of 40 nautical miles from VOR 'DUB' should be expressed as DUB180040.'

question_text: In Appendix 3, what does the Alignment Function (two Carriage Returns followed by one Line Feed) accomplish?

options.B: It begins a new line on page copy to avoid exceeding 69 characters per line

explanation: Appendix 3, 1.5.5: 'When the standard ATS messages are prepared in teletypewriter form, an Alignment Function (two Carriage Returns followed by one Line Feed) shall be inserted: ... whenever it is necessary to begin a new line on page copy (see Note). In such cases, the Alignment Function shall be inserted between two data elements and shall not divide an element.' The Note refers to the 69-character line limit.

question_text: What is the AFTN priority indicator for a message concerning a flight plan modification (CHG)?
explanation: Chapter 11, 11.1.3: 'Movement and control messages (FF)' includes modification messages (CHG). So FF is the priority indicator.

question_text: In Appendix 5, what is the response attribute for a downlink message that requires a response and has precedence 1?
explanation: Table 14-3 shows downlink response attribute 'Y' means 'Yes' a response is required, and precedence 1.

question_text: What is the CPDLC message element for 'REQUEST DIRECT TO (position)'?
explanation: Table A5-1-2, Route downlinks: RTED-1 is 'Request for a direct clearance to the specified position. REQUEST DIRECT TO (position)'

question_text: What is the purpose of the AIDC 'Coordinate Reject' message?

options.B: To indicate that the proposed coordination is unacceptable and no counterproposal is being made

explanation: Appendix 6, paragraph 2.6: 'Coordinate Reject message is used to indicate that the proposed coordination, or revision thereto, is unacceptable and no counterproposal is being proposed. Any existing coordination conditions remain as previously coordinated.'

question_text: What is the responsibility of the pilot when a special air-report is required due to severe turbulence?

options.B: Make the report as soon as practicable after the phenomenon is encountered
options.C: Wait for the next routine position report
options.D: Report only if requested by ATC
options.E: Report only if the turbulence lasts more than 5 minutes

explanation: Appendix 1, Section 1.4: 'Special air-reports shall be made as soon as practicable after a phenomenon calling for a special air-report has been observed.'

question_text: What is the required content of a position report for an aircraft on a route not defined by designated significant points?

options.A: Report over each compulsory reporting point
options.B: Report as soon as possible after the first half hour of flight and at hourly intervals thereafter

explanation: The document states: 'On routes not defined by designated significant points, position reports shall be made by the aircraft as soon as possible after the first half hour of flight and at hourly intervals thereafter, except as provided in 4.11.1.3.' (Chapter 4, 4.11.1.2)

question_text: What is the maximum time after take-off that an aircraft experiencing communication failure in IMC should continue at last assigned speed and level before adjusting?
explanation: The document states: 'unless otherwise prescribed on the basis of a regional air navigation agreement, in airspace where procedural separation is being applied, maintain the last assigned speed and level, or minimum flight altitude if higher, for a period of 20 minutes following the aircraft's failure to report its position over a compulsory reporting point and thereafter adjust level and speed in accordance with the filed flight plan.' (Chapter 15, 15.3.3 b) 1))

question_text: What is the minimum horizontal separation between aircraft on parallel tracks when using RNP 4 and SLOP up to 2 NM is authorized?
explanation: Table 5-2 shows for RNP 4 with SLOP up to 2 NM authorized, the minimum spacing between tracks is 42.6 km (23 NM).

question_text: What is the minimum wake turbulence separation time for a LIGHT aircraft landing behind a HEAVY aircraft?
explanation: The document states: 'LIGHT aircraft landing behind a HEAVY or MEDIUM aircraft — 3 minutes.' (Chapter 5, 5.8.2.1 e))

question_text: What is the maximum number of characters allowed for a significant point coded designator?
explanation: Appendix 2, Item 15 (c)(2) Significant point: 'The coded designator (2 to 5 characters) assigned to the point (e.g. LN, MAY, HADDY)'

question_text: What is the primary responsibility of the aerodrome control tower regarding runway incursions?

options.A: To report any occurrence involving an obstruction on the runway or a runway incursion
options.E: To close the runway after an incursion

explanation: The document states: 'Pilots and air traffic controllers shall report any occurrence involving an obstruction on the runway or a runway incursion.' (Chapter 7, 7.4.1.4.2)

question_text: What is the phraseology to cancel a take-off clearance?

options.B: HOLD POSITION, CANCEL TAKE-OFF I SAY AGAIN CANCEL TAKE-OFF (reasons)

explanation: The document states: '... to cancel a take-off clearance e) HOLD POSITION, CANCEL TAKE-OFF I SAY AGAIN CANCEL TAKE-OFF (reasons);' (Chapter 12, 12.3.4.11)

question_text: What is the phraseology to instruct an aircraft to resume published speed?
explanation: Under ATS surveillance phraseologies, speed control: 'k) RESUME PUBLISHED SPEED;' (Chapter 12, 12.4.1.6)

question_text: What is the maximum number of simultaneous ADS-C agreements an aircraft must support according to Chapter 13?
explanation: Chapter 13, 13.4.3.4.1.5: 'The aircraft must be capable of supporting ADS-C agreements with at least four ATC unit ADS-C ground systems simultaneously.'

question_text: What is the minimum level change for considering that an aircraft has vacated a level for ADS-C purposes?
explanation: Chapter 13, 13.5.2.3: 'An aircraft cleared to leave a level is considered to have commenced its manoeuvre and vacated the previously occupied level when the ADS level information indicates a change of more than 90 m (300 ft) in the anticipated direction from its previously assigned level, or verification has been made by receipt of a CPDLC or voice report from the pilot.'

question_text: When an ADS-C ground system attempts to establish an agreement but the aircraft cannot support another contract, what information should the aircraft provide?

options.B: The ICAO location indicators of the ground systems with which it currently has contracts

explanation: Chapter 13, 13.4.3.4.1.5.1: 'When an ADS-C ground system attempts to establish an ADS-C agreement with an aircraft and is unable to do so due to the aircraft's inability to support an additional ADS contract, the aircraft should reply with the ICAO location indicators or eight-letter facility indicators of the ground systems with which it currently has contracts, in order for the ATC unit to negotiate a contract release.'

question_text: What is the phraseology to instruct an aircraft to resume normal CPDLC operations after a stoppage?
explanation: Chapter 14, 14.3.9.2: 'The resumption of the normal use of CPDLC shall be advised using the following phrase: ((call sign) or ALL STATIONS) RESUME NORMAL CPDLC OPERATIONS'

question_text: What action should a controller take when a CPDLC emergency message is received?

options.B: Acknowledge receipt by the most efficient means available
options.E: Request the pilot to confirm by voice
options.F: Transfer the communication to another sector

explanation: Chapter 14, 14.3.5.1: 'When a CPDLC emergency message is received, the controller shall acknowledge receipt of the message by the most efficient means available.'

question_text: What is the maximum time after which an aircraft in communication failure in IMC and under radar control should maintain last assigned level before adjusting?
explanation: Chapter 15, 15.3.3 b) 2): 'in airspace where an ATS surveillance system is used in the provision of air traffic control, maintain the last assigned speed and level, or minimum flight altitude if higher, for a period of 7 minutes following: i) the time the last assigned level or minimum flight altitude is reached; or ii) the time the transponder is set to Code 7600 or the ADS-B transmitter is set to indicate the loss of air-ground communications; or iii) the aircraft's failure to report its position over a compulsory reporting point; whichever is later and thereafter adjust level and speed in accordance with the filed flight plan.'

question_text: What is the primary objective of the short-term conflict alert (STCA) function?

options.B: To assist the controller in preventing collision between aircraft

explanation: Chapter 15, 15.7.2 Note 1: 'The objective of the STCA function is to assist the controller in preventing collision between aircraft by generating, in a timely manner, an alert of a potential or actual infringement of separation minima.'

question_text: What is the minimum temperature correction required for minimum vectoring altitude?

options.A: No correction specified, but low temperature effect shall be corrected when necessary

explanation: Chapter 8, 8.6.5.2: 'When necessary, the relevant minimum vectoring altitude shall include a correction for low temperature effect.' No specific value is mandated; it is left to the appropriate authority.

question_text: What is the meaning of the term 'Unidentified aircraft'?

options.A: An aircraft that has not filed a flight plan
options.B: An aircraft which has been observed or reported to be operating in a given area but whose identity has not been established
options.C: An aircraft with a faulty transponder
options.D: An aircraft that is not in communication
options.E: An aircraft that is not on a flight plan route
options.F: An aircraft that has been stolen

explanation: Chapter 15, 15.5.1 Note 1: 'Unidentified aircraft. An aircraft which has been observed or reported to be operating in a given area but whose identity has not been established.'

question_text: What action should an ATS unit take when it learns that an aircraft is being intercepted in its area of responsibility?

options.B: Attempt to establish two-way communication with the intercepted aircraft, inform the pilot, contact the intercept control unit, relay messages, and ensure safety
options.C: Instruct the intercepted aircraft to squawk 7700
options.D: Request the intercepting aircraft to break off
options.E: Transfer control to the military immediately

explanation: Chapter 15, 15.5.2.1: 'As soon as an air traffic services unit learns that an aircraft is being intercepted in its area of responsibility, it shall take such of the following steps as are appropriate: a) attempt to establish two-way communication... b) inform the pilot of the interception; c) establish contact with the intercept control unit... d) relay messages... e) in close coordination... take all necessary steps to ensure the safety of the intercepted aircraft; f) inform ATS units serving adjacent FIRs...'

question_text: What is the recommended escape manoeuvre for an aircraft that has encountered a volcanic ash cloud?

options.C: Reverse course and begin a descent if terrain permits
options.D: Increase speed to outrun the ash

explanation: Chapter 15, 15.8.1 Note 1: 'Experience has shown that the recommended escape manoeuvre for an aircraft which has encountered an ash cloud is to reverse its course and begin a descent if terrain permits.'

question_text: What information should be included in a NOTAM for a planned shutdown of CPDLC?

options.A: The shutdown period and, if necessary, the voice communication frequencies to be used
options.D: The aircraft that will be affected

explanation: Chapter 14, 14.3.7.1: 'When a system shutdown of the communications network or the CPDLC ground system is planned, a NOTAM shall be published to inform all affected parties of the shutdown period and, if necessary, the details of the voice communication frequencies to be used.'

question_text: What is the meaning of the phrase 'WHEN READY (instruction)' in ATC phraseology?

options.A: The instruction must be executed immediately
options.B: The instruction must be executed at the time of the pilot's convenience
options.D: The instruction will be given later
options.E: The pilot must report when ready

explanation: Chapter 12, 12.3.1.2, under level changes: '... to require action when convenient m) WHEN READY (instruction);'

question_text: What is the correct phraseology for a pilot to report that the aircraft is established on the track between two significant points with zero offset?

options.B: ESTABLISHED ON THE TRACK WITH ZERO OFFSET
options.D: ESTABLISHED ON THE TRACK BETWEEN (point) AND (point) [WITH ZERO OFFSET]

explanation: Chapter 12, 12.3.2.8: '*h) ESTABLISHED ON THE TRACK;' but the full phrase from instruction is: 'CONFIRM ESTABLISHED ON THE TRACK BETWEEN (significant point) AND (significant point) [WITH ZERO OFFSET];' and the pilot response can be 'ESTABLISHED ON THE TRACK BETWEEN (significant point) AND (significant point) [WITH ZERO OFFSET]' or just 'ESTABLISHED ON THE TRACK'. The most complete correct answer includes the points and zero offset if required.

question_text: What is the meaning of the phrase 'CHECK STUCK MICROPHONE (frequency)'?

options.A: The controller instructs the pilot to check for a stuck microphone on the specified frequency due to a continuous transmission
options.B: The pilot is asked to change microphone
options.C: The controller reports a stuck microphone
options.D: The pilot reports a stuck microphone
options.F: The controller requests a radio check

question_text: What is the phraseology to request a pilot to squawk Mode C?
explanation: Chapter 12, 12.4.3.11: 'To request transmission of pressure-altitude: a) SQUAWK CHARLIE;'

question_text: What is the correct phraseology for a pilot to report an emergency descent?
explanation: Chapter 12, 12.3.2.5: 'Emergency descent *a) EMERGENCY DESCENT (intentions);'

question_text: What is the phraseology to instruct an aircraft to make a short approach?
explanation: Chapter 12, 12.3.4.15: 'a) MAKE SHORT APPROACH;'

question_text: What is the phraseology for a pilot to request a low pass?
explanation: Chapter 12, 12.3.4.16: '*g) REQUEST LOW PASS (reasons);'

question_text: What is the phraseology to instruct an aircraft to stop squawking altitude information due to faulty operation?
explanation: Chapter 12, 12.4.3.13: 'To request termination of pressure-altitude transmission because of faulty operation a) STOP SQUAWK CHARLIE WRONG INDICATION;'

question_text: What is the responsibility of the operator regarding RPL changes of a temporary nature (e.g., delay of 30 minutes or more)?

options.A: Notify the ATS unit responsible for the departure aerodrome immediately
options.C: Do nothing, as RPL automatically adjusts

explanation: Chapter 16, 16.4.4.2.3: 'Whenever it is expected by the operator that a specific flight, for which an RPL has been submitted, is likely to encounter a delay of 30 minutes or more in excess of the off-block time stated in that flight plan, the ATS unit responsible for the departure aerodrome shall be notified immediately.'

question_text: What is the minimum lead time for submitting complete RPL listings for international flights?
explanation: Chapter 16, 16.4.3.2: 'The minimum lead time required for the submission of such listings shall be established by the administrations concerned and published in their AIPs. This minimum lead time shall be at least two weeks.' Two weeks = 14 days. So 14 days. But option E is 14 days. However, the question says 'minimum lead time for submitting complete RPL listings' and the answer is 14 days. But option C is 7 days, D is 10, E is 14. So correct is E. But wait, the text says 'at least two weeks' = 14 days. So answer E.

question_text: What is the maximum strategic lateral offset (SLOP) allowed when lateral separation minima are between 6 NM and 15 NM?
explanation: Chapter 16, 16.5.2 c): 'where the lateral separation minima or spacing between route centre lines is 11.1 km (6 NM) or more and less than 28 km (15 NM), offsets to the right of the centre line relative to the direction of flight in tenths of a nautical mile up to a maximum of 0.9 km (0.5 NM).'

question_text: Who is responsible for the decision to apply a strategic lateral offset?
explanation: Chapter 16, 16.5.4: 'The decision to apply a strategic lateral offset shall be the responsibility of the flight crew.'

question_text: What information should the flight crew provide to ATS when a suspected communicable disease is on board, regarding the number of persons on board?

options.A: Only the number of suspected cases
options.B: Total number of persons on board and number of suspected cases
options.D: Only the nature of the disease

explanation: Chapter 16, 16.6.1: 'e) number of persons on board; f) number of suspected case(s) on board;' Both are required.

question_text: What is the definition of 'Fly-by waypoint'?

options.A: A waypoint at which a turn is initiated in order to join the next segment
options.B: A waypoint which requires turn anticipation to allow tangential interception of the next segment
options.C: A waypoint that must be overflown before turning
options.D: A waypoint that is only used for departure procedures
options.E: A waypoint that is used for en-route navigation only
options.F: A waypoint that is defined by latitude and longitude

explanation: The document defines: 'Fly-by waypoint. A waypoint which requires turn anticipation to allow tangential interception of the next segment of a route or procedure.' (Definitions section)

question_text: What is the definition of 'Flyover waypoint'?

options.A: A waypoint which requires turn anticipation
options.B: A waypoint at which a turn is initiated in order to join the next segment
options.C: A waypoint that must be crossed before turning
options.D: A waypoint used only for arrival procedures
options.E: A waypoint that is not used in RNAV
options.F: A waypoint that is defined by bearing and distance

explanation: The document defines: 'Flyover waypoint. A waypoint at which a turn is initiated in order to join the next segment of a route or procedure.'

question_text: What is the purpose of the 'D-ATIS' service?
explanation: The document defines: 'Data link-automatic terminal information service (D-ATIS). The provision of ATIS via data link.' (Definitions section)

question_text: What is the meaning of 'Position symbol'?

options.A: The visual indication of the position of an aircraft on a situation display obtained after automatic processing of positional data
options.E: The map symbol for a waypoint

explanation: The document defines: 'Position symbol. The visual indication in symbolic form, on a situation display, of the position of an aircraft, aerodrome vehicle or other object, obtained after automatic processing of positional data derived from any source.' (Definitions section)

question_text: What is the definition of 'Performance-based communication (PBC)'?

options.B: Communication based on performance specifications applied to the provision of air traffic services
options.E: Communication that is independent of aircraft equipment

explanation: The document defines: 'Performance-based communication (PBC). Communication based on performance specifications applied to the provision of air traffic services.' (Definitions section)

question_text: What is the definition of 'Ground effect' for helicopters?

options.A: The condition of improved performance due to the interference of the surface with the airflow pattern of the rotor system when operating near the ground
options.B: The reduction in lift near the ground
options.C: The effect of ground proximity on navigation
options.D: The interference of terrain with radio signals
options.F: The effect of temperature on rotor efficiency

explanation: The document defines: 'Ground effect. A condition of improved performance (lift) due to the interference of the surface with the airflow pattern of the rotor system when a helicopter or other VTOL aircraft is operating near the ground.' (Definitions section)
